title: Ophiopogon tawadae
taxonomy: Ophiopogon tawadae is classified within the family Asparagaceae, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes lilies and grasses. It belongs to the genus Ophiopogon, a group of evergreen, perennial plants commonly known as Mondo grass or Lilyturf. The species Ophiopogon tawadae is native to Japan and is often cultivated for its attractive foliage and ability to thrive in shaded environments. Within the genus Ophiopogon, Ophiopogon tawadae is a specific species that is valued for its unique characteristics and growth habits. Ophiopogon tawadae is often used in landscaping and horticulture due to its ability to tolerate a wide range of conditions and its attractive, grass-like appearance.
